rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Other issues noted in the petition, courtesy copy of which was not provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B: (1) The opening paragraph does not make sense; (2) this is purportedly a petition on waiver of account, but there are several references to an account; (3) the sole beneficiary of the will is the decedent's revocable trust, not Ray Lee Thompson, Jr.; (4) the successor co-trustees were reported to be Ray Thompson and Juanetta Swetting, who must both waive an accounting by the executor unless she has declined in writing to serve as such successor co-trustee; (5) there were purportedly personal property taxes paid by the estate, when there was just cash inventoried; (6) there is reportedly no cash balance in the estate (?!); (7) there is no Exhibit D attached to the petition; and (8) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.] The proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WINSTON R. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an Examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Because of the delay in closing estate administration and some of the allegations in Administrator's report of status, the hearing was not continued automatically for another month. 1) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] 2) Administrator has not yet marshaled (?) or inventoried the decedent's gun collection more than three years after Decedent's death and more than a year after Letters were issued to Administrator. No Supplemental I&A has been filed yet. Probate Code Section 8800(b) requires the I&A to be filed within four months from the date Letters are issued to a general personal representative. That would have been October 3, 2021. 3) If Administrator knew there were guns to be marshaled and appraised, the "Final" I&A he filed on 6/6/22 should have been a Partial No. 1. He alleges in this report that it was a "part 1" but it was not. 4) The only prayer in this report of status is for approval of the administrator's acts. He does not disclose the estimated time to close the estate or request additional time for estate administration, which information the sole heir is entitled to know. 5) The notice of hearing did not include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA SCHOENFELD SEBASTIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/18/2023 in light of counsel's declaration filed on 1/20/2023.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The report required when an account has been waived must list the information required by law, including information as to sales, purchases, or exchanges of assets and changes in the form of assets. [CRC 7.550(b).] What happened to the other assets listed on Attachment 2 of the I&A? An amount well in excess of the FDIC insured amount is being maintained in one checking account. A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] There was no (federal firearms} license attached to Exhibit C. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/3/23. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
